spec_match.rationale: Tree maps the four `err.name` values the spec exposes (AbortError, OperationError, NotSupportedError, fall-through) to the correct merchant action — matches the contract.

interactive.rationale: Install + canMakePayment + three outcome buttons invoke a real handler and populate an action card, but only Success / AbortError / OperationError are runnable — the NotSupportedError and SecurityError nodes are visual only, never highlighted live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises `run a scenario to see where the real browser result routes`. The runner only routes three of the four branches; the NotSupportedError/SecurityError branches are static and never light up.

edge_cases.rationale: Visual coverage of fall-through cases is good, but no probe actually triggers them. Also no demonstration of what happens if `err.name` is undefined / non-string (legacy or proxy DOMException) — the page assumes the four-name model holds.

references.rationale: see-also lists only sibling concepts — the chromestatus.com/feature/5942637229113344 link is missing on this page, violating the self-heal invariant.

design_system.rationale: Variables for all colours, button colour overridden alongside background where applied, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: see-also has only siblings. Add https://chromestatus.com/feature/5942637229113344 to keep the slug-recovery script working.

openQuestions.title: Light up the NotSupportedError branch from a real probe
openQuestions.detail: Instantiate PaymentRequest with a method URL that isn't registered — that rejects with NotSupportedError before `show()` and routes to the fourth branch. The visitor can then see all four discriminator paths exercised from real browser code, not just three.

openQuestions.title: Trigger SecurityError via missing user activation
openQuestions.detail: Calling show() outside a user gesture (e.g. from a setTimeout) yields SecurityError on some configurations. A `simulate broken caller` button would surface that branch.

openQuestions.title: Highlight the routed node in the tree
openQuestions.detail: When a scenario runs, only the action card updates. The tree nodes themselves don't visually highlight which branch was taken. A short animation that flashes the chosen node would make the routing legible.

openQuestions.title: Show the literal `if/else if` ladder in code form
openQuestions.detail: The tree is the visual; pair it with a four-branch code snippet that matches one-to-one, so a developer can copy the pattern. Currently the routing logic lives only in the JS source.

summary: Conceptually the clearest piece in this feature set, but only three of the four visualised branches are live — and the page misses the chromestatus reference link. Light up the static branches with real probes and the demo becomes the canonical reference.
